call_transcript:
Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Finance Solutions. I see you recently filled out a form online regarding a Personal Loan. Um, how are you doing today? 
Eleanor: Good, thanks. 
Agent: Great! So, we're pre-approving you for a $10,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 8.99%.  That's right, 8.99%.  Now, what were you hoping to use the funds for, if you don't mind me asking? 
Eleanor: Just some home improvements, you know, a new bathroom. 
Agent: Excellent! A bathroom remodel, fantastic.  And are you currently carrying any other debts? Just to get a full picture. 
Eleanor: A little student loan debt, but it's manageable. 
Agent: Okay, that's good.  So, with this $10,000 Personal Loan at 8.99%, your monthly payments would be approximately $198.  We can also look at different terms, if you'd like.  We have 36, 48, and 60-month options. 
Eleanor: Hmm, $198 sounds pretty good. What are the fees like? 
Agent: There's a one-time origination fee of $150.  That's it, really. No prepayment penalties. 
Eleanor: Okay. That's…reasonable. 
Agent: Wonderful! So, are you ready to proceed with the application? It's a quick online process. 
Eleanor: Yeah, okay, let's do it.

Agent: Hello, Mr. Miller? This is Sarah calling from Financial Freedom. We're offering a special promotion on our Signature Credit Card. 
Robert: Where did you get my number? I didn't give anyone permission to call me. 
Agent: Sir, we… uh… it's a marketing list. We're offering a $2500 Signature Credit Card with an interest rate of 19.99%. 
Robert: 19.99?! Are you kidding me? That's outrageous! I'm already on a fixed income. 
Agent: Well, sir, it depends on your credit score… 
Robert: (Interrupting) I don't need a credit card! I'm perfectly fine with what I have. Don't call me again! (Click)

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Secure Finance. I'm calling regarding a Personal Loan. Your friend, Maria Rodriguez, referred you. 
Jessica: Oh, hi! Yeah, Maria mentioned you guys. I'm thinking about consolidating some debt. 
Agent: Excellent! We can definitely help with that. We are pre-approving you for a $15,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 12.5%.  That's 12.5%, Jessica. What kind of debt are we looking at consolidating? 
Jessica: Mostly credit card debt, about $12,000. 
Agent: Okay, great. So, with a $15,000 loan at 12.5%, your monthly payments would be around $315. That's significantly lower than what you're likely paying in credit card interest. 
Jessica: Hmm, that sounds good, but I'm worried about the fees. 
Agent: There's a $200 origination fee, but no prepayment penalties. We also offer a rate discount if you set up automatic payments. 
Jessica: What if I pay it off early? 
Agent: No penalty at all! You can pay it off whenever you want without any extra charges. 
Jessica: Okay. And what's the APR? 
Agent: The APR is 13.2%, factoring in the origination fee. 
Jessica: Okay, that's… still a bit higher than I was hoping for. 
Agent: I understand. Let me see if there's anything we can do. (Typing sounds) Okay, I can offer you a slightly longer term, 60 months, which would lower your monthly payment to $280, but the APR would be 13.5%. 
Jessica: Let me think about it. Can you send me some information via email? 
Agent: Absolutely! I'll send you a detailed breakdown of the loan terms and a link to the online application.  My email is david.securefinance@email.com.

Agent: Hello, Michael? This is Lisa calling from Premier Financial. I see you expressed interest in our Signature Credit Card online. 
Michael: Yeah, I was just browsing. 
Agent: Great! We're offering a $5000 Signature Credit Card with a 17.99% interest rate. It has great rewards points, cash back, and a low annual fee. 
Michael: 17.99%? That's a little high, isn't it? 
Agent: It is, but it comes with a lot of benefits. You earn 2% cash back on all purchases, and we have a 0% introductory APR for the first six months. 
Michael: Hmm, 0% for six months… that's interesting. What's the annual fee? 
Agent: $99 a year. But the rewards you'll earn will easily offset that. 
Michael: Okay. I'm still thinking about it. 
Agent: Absolutely. I can email you a brochure with all the details. Would you like that? 
Michael: Yeah, that would be great. My email is michael.davis@email.com.

Agent: Hello, Ms. Rodriguez? This is Kevin calling from Liberty Loans. 
Patricia: (Immediately angry) You guys called me *yesterday*! I told you I wasn't interested! Where did you even get my number?! 
Agent: Ma'am, I apologize for the inconvenience. We're offering a $3000 Personal Loan at 24.99%. 
Patricia: 24.99?! Are you serious? I can't afford that! I'm unemployed, for goodness sake! 
Agent: Well, we do consider all applicants… 
Patricia: (Interrupting) I don't want your loan! Take me off your list! I'm filing a complaint! Don't you dare call me again! (Slammed phone down)
